trans-unit.source: The Print layout
trans-unit.target: The Print layout

trans-unit.source: The print layout in QGIS is where you design and finalise the map in order to print or export it as a PDF (or file format of your choice). Here you can add important elements such as the legend, title, explanatory text, and anything you need to create a comprehensive map. By adding layout elements (legend, title, scale bar, sources, etc.) to a map, you provide your audience with the necessary information to contextualise and evaluate the information shown on the map.
trans-unit.target: The print layout in QGIS is where you design and finalise the map in order to print or export it as a PDF (or file format of your choice). Here you can add important elements such as the legend, title, explanatory text, and anything you need to create a comprehensive map. By adding layout elements (legend, title, scale bar, sources, etc.) to a map, you provide your audience with the necessary information to contextualise and evaluate the information shown on the map.

trans-unit.source: In the top bar, go to __`Project` → `New Print Layout` → enter a name for the new print layout → click `OK`__.
trans-unit.target: In the top bar, go to __`Project` → `New Print Layout` → enter a name for the new print layout → click `OK`__.

trans-unit.source: A new window with a blank print layout will appear.
trans-unit.target: A new window with a blank print layout will appear.

trans-unit.source: Create a new Print Layout :::
trans-unit.target: Create a new Print Layout :::

trans-unit.source: Map Composition
trans-unit.target: Map Composition

trans-unit.source: A good map guides the reader in understanding the information available on the map, makes the information easily accessible, and is not overloaded with information.
trans-unit.target: A good map guides the reader in understanding the information available on the map, makes the information easily accessible, and is not overloaded with information.

trans-unit.source: In general, there are a few things to keep in mind when creating a map:
trans-unit.target: In general, there are a few things to keep in mind when creating a map:

trans-unit.source: The main map should cover the largest portion of the page and be centred.
trans-unit.target: The main map should cover the largest portion of the page and be centred.

trans-unit.source: A complete map must have a legend, sources, title, scale, and necessary other information to contextualize the information presented on the map.
trans-unit.target: A complete map must have a legend, sources, title, scale, and necessary other information to contextualize the information presented on the map.

trans-unit.source: Additional information, such as title, sources, scale bar, legend, orientation, etc. should be scaled accordingly.
trans-unit.target: Additional information, such as title, sources, scale bar, legend, orientation, etc. should be scaled accordingly.

trans-unit.source: Titles should be large so the reader can identify it as the main topic of the map.
trans-unit.target: Titles should be large so the reader can identify it as the main topic of the map.

trans-unit.source: Additional information should be smaller and moved out of the main focus of the page (e.g. at the bottom, to the sides, or in the corners).
trans-unit.target: Additional information should be smaller and moved out of the main focus of the page (e.g. at the bottom, to the sides, or in the corners).

trans-unit.source: A well-structured page layout helps the reader discern the different information on the map and makes it easier to know where to look for certain information. Frames and boxes can structure the page layout. For example, a legend can be put on the bottom or to the right of the map.
trans-unit.target: A well-structured page layout helps the reader discern the different information on the map and makes it easier to know where to look for certain information. Frames and boxes can structure the page layout. For example, a legend can be put on the bottom or to the right of the map.

trans-unit.source: In order to produce good maps, there are some __basic rules__ to follow and __semiological mistakes__ to avoid. The following subchapter will go over the key elements of a map as well as common design mistakes.
trans-unit.target: In order to produce good maps, there are some __basic rules__ to follow and __semiological mistakes__ to avoid. The following subchapter will go over the key elements of a map as well as common design mistakes.

trans-unit.source: Key elements of a map
trans-unit.target: Key elements of a map

trans-unit.source: In order to provide your audience and readers with sufficient information so they can contextualise the map, it is important to add these key map elements:
trans-unit.target: In order to provide your audience and readers with sufficient information so they can contextualise the map, it is important to add these key map elements:

trans-unit.source: __Title__
trans-unit.target: __Title__

trans-unit.source: __Legend__
trans-unit.target: __Legend__

trans-unit.source: __Scale__
trans-unit.target: __Scale__

trans-unit.source: __Orientation__
trans-unit.target: __Orientation__

trans-unit.source: __Source__
trans-unit.target: __Source__

trans-unit.source: __Overview Map__
trans-unit.target: __Overview Map__

trans-unit.source: __Author__
trans-unit.target: __Author__

trans-unit.source: Elements of good map composition :::
trans-unit.target: Elements of good map composition :::

trans-unit.source: __The title__ summarises in a few words the information represented on the map, giving the reader useful contextual information. Titles should include the following information:
trans-unit.target: __The title__ summarises in a few words the information represented on the map, giving the reader useful contextual information. Titles should include the following information:

trans-unit.source: __The place__, with several degrees of precision according to the scale (Country, Region, Township, ...)
trans-unit.target: __The place__, with several degrees of precision according to the scale (Country, Region, Township, ...)

trans-unit.source: __The subject__ intelligible by all (make sure that any acronyms used are specified elsewhere on the map)
trans-unit.target: __The subject__ intelligible by all (make sure that any acronyms used are specified elsewhere on the map)

trans-unit.source: __the date__ of the represented data
trans-unit.target: __the date__ of the represented data

trans-unit.source: _Examples:_
trans-unit.target: _Examples:_

trans-unit.source: _"Access to health care in Maputo, Mozambique in 2022"_
trans-unit.target: _"Access to health care in Maputo, Mozambique in 2022"_

trans-unit.source: _"Flooding Risk in Ghardaïa, Algeria"_
trans-unit.target: _"Flooding Risk in Ghardaïa, Algeria"_

trans-unit.source: __The legend__ is key to interpreting the information represented on the map. Without it, it is impossible to understand the meaning of the different symbols and colours used on the map. In order to guide the reader, the legend must be:
trans-unit.target: __The legend__ is key to interpreting the information represented on the map. Without it, it is impossible to understand the meaning of the different symbols and colours used on the map. In order to guide the reader, the legend must be:

trans-unit.source: __Comprehensive__: All the data on the map must be presented in the legend.
trans-unit.target: __Comprehensive__: All the data on the map must be presented in the legend.

trans-unit.source: __Representative__: The figures on the map and in the legend must match (same size, same color, ...).
trans-unit.target: __Representative__: The figures on the map and in the legend must match (same size, same color, ...).

trans-unit.source: __Organized__: The data in the legend can be grouped by thematic categories (health, environment, background map, ...) or by type of figure (point, line, surface) to facilitate reading.
trans-unit.target: __Organized__: The data in the legend can be grouped by thematic categories (health, environment, background map, ...) or by type of figure (point, line, surface) to facilitate reading.

trans-unit.source: Example of a well organized legend :::
trans-unit.target: Example of a well organized legend :::

trans-unit.source: __The scale bar__ is essential to a map since it gives the correspondence between a distance measured on the map and the distance in the real world. There are two types of scales:
trans-unit.target: __The scale bar__ is essential to a map since it gives the correspondence between a distance measured on the map and the distance in the real world. There are two types of scales:

trans-unit.source: __The numerical scale__ is expressed as a fraction (1/25000 or 1:25000) that indicates the ratio between 1 centimetre on the map and the actual distance. It is a scale that can be calculated with GIS software, and is often found in topographic maps. A scale of 1:25000 means that 1 cm represents 25,000 cm (or 250 meters) on the ground.
trans-unit.target: __The numerical scale__ is expressed as a fraction (1/25000 or 1:25000) that indicates the ratio between 1 centimetre on the map and the actual distance. It is a scale that can be calculated with GIS software, and is often found in topographic maps. A scale of 1:25000 means that 1 cm represents 25,000 cm (or 250 meters) on the ground.

trans-unit.source: __The graphical scale__ is expressed by a line on the map, with an associated distance value. This scale is very useful for understanding distances on the ground. The graphical scale will always be the correct size, even if a different printing format is used, since it will undergo the same transformation as the rest of the map
trans-unit.target: __The graphical scale__ is expressed by a line on the map, with an associated distance value. This scale is very useful for understanding distances on the ground. The graphical scale will always be the correct size, even if a different printing format is used, since it will undergo the same transformation as the rest of the map

trans-unit.source: Scale bar examples :::
trans-unit.target: Scale bar examples :::

trans-unit.source: Orientation
trans-unit.target: Orientation

trans-unit.source: Even though the majority of the maps are oriented towards the north, it is still necessary to specify the orientation of your map. This is often indicated by an arrow pointing to the north, as sometimes a non-northwards orientation is used to represent the study area.
trans-unit.target: Even though the majority of the maps are oriented towards the north, it is still necessary to specify the orientation of your map. This is often indicated by an arrow pointing to the north, as sometimes a non-northwards orientation is used to represent the study area.

trans-unit.source: Sources
trans-unit.target: Sources

trans-unit.source: Any data represented on a map should have its sources indicated. This provides a record of the data used, but also credits the author of the data. The reader will then be able to look for more information on the sources if he wishes. Open access geographic data, such as OpenStreetMap, are increasingly populated and must also be cited on maps.
trans-unit.target: Any data represented on a map should have its sources indicated. This provides a record of the data used, but also credits the author of the data. The reader will then be able to look for more information on the sources if he wishes. Open access geographic data, such as OpenStreetMap, are increasingly populated and must also be cited on maps.

trans-unit.source: It is possible to give the source of each data under the legend, or to do so in a dedicated space in the map. The level of precision of the sources varies according to the author or the precision of the data.
trans-unit.target: It is possible to give the source of each data under the legend, or to do so in a dedicated space in the map. The level of precision of the sources varies according to the author or the precision of the data.

trans-unit.source: :::{admonition} Now it's your turn! :class: tip
trans-unit.target: :::{admonition} Now it's your turn! :class: tip

trans-unit.source: Take a look at the maps below and pay close attention to how the cartographers arranged the different elements. You can also take a look at maps you encountered in your work or daily life.
trans-unit.target: Take a look at the maps below and pay close attention to how the cartographers arranged the different elements. You can also take a look at maps you encountered in your work or daily life.

trans-unit.source: ::::{dropdown} __Map Example 1__
trans-unit.target: ::::{dropdown} __Map Example 1__

trans-unit.source: Flood affected areas and roads in the Somali Region, Ethiopia (Source: OCHA) :::
trans-unit.target: Flood affected areas and roads in the Somali Region, Ethiopia (Source: OCHA) :::

trans-unit.source: ::::{dropdown} __Map Example 2__
trans-unit.target: ::::{dropdown} __Map Example 2__

trans-unit.source: Internally Displaced Persons (IDPs), 30 September 2024 (Source: [UNHCR](https://reliefweb.int/map/sudan/regional-bureau-east-horn-africa-and-great-lakes-region-internally-displaced-persons-idps-30-september-2024)). :::
trans-unit.target: Internally Displaced Persons (IDPs), 30 September 2024 (Source: [UNHCR](https://reliefweb.int/map/sudan/regional-bureau-east-horn-africa-and-great-lakes-region-internally-displaced-persons-idps-30-september-2024)). :::

trans-unit.source: ::::{dropdown} __Map Example 3__
trans-unit.target: ::::{dropdown} __Map Example 3__

trans-unit.source: South Sudan: Humanitarian Situation Monitoring, April-May 2024 - Damaged shelters (Source: [REACH](https://repository.impact-initiatives.org/document/impact/897badb8/REACH_SSD_Map_HSM_AprilMay2024_DamagedShelters_June2024-1.pdf)) :::
trans-unit.target: South Sudan: Humanitarian Situation Monitoring, April-May 2024 - Damaged shelters (Source: [REACH](https://repository.impact-initiatives.org/document/impact/897badb8/REACH_SSD_Map_HSM_AprilMay2024_DamagedShelters_June2024-1.pdf)) :::

trans-unit.source: ::::{dropdown} __Map Example 4__
trans-unit.target: ::::{dropdown} __Map Example 4__

trans-unit.source: Operational overview or response activity map (Source: [Shelter Cluster Vanuata](https://reliefweb.int/map/vanuatu/vanuatu-tropical-cyclone-lola-distribution-and-gap-map-malampa-13022024)) :::
trans-unit.target: Operational overview or response activity map (Source: [Shelter Cluster Vanuata](https://reliefweb.int/map/vanuatu/vanuatu-tropical-cyclone-lola-distribution-and-gap-map-malampa-13022024)) :::

trans-unit.source: Now that we have covered what to keep in mind when designing maps, let's take a look at how to create maps with the print layout composer in QGIS.
trans-unit.target: Now that we have covered what to keep in mind when designing maps, let's take a look at how to create maps with the print layout composer in QGIS.

trans-unit.source: Self-Assessment Questions
trans-unit.target: Self-Assessment Questions

trans-unit.source: ::::{admonition} Check your knowledge :class: note
trans-unit.target: ::::{admonition} Check your knowledge :class: note

trans-unit.source: __What is the print layout manager and why do you use it to create maps in QGIS instead of the main QGIS window?__
trans-unit.target: __What is the print layout manager and why do you use it to create maps in QGIS instead of the main QGIS window?__

trans-unit.source: :::{dropdown} Answer The Print Layout Manager (via `Project` → `New Print Layout` in QGIS) is a separate interface/window in which you assemble your map for output (print or export) rather than working only in the main map canvas.
trans-unit.target: :::{dropdown} Answer The Print Layout Manager (via `Project` → `New Print Layout` in QGIS) is a separate interface/window in which you assemble your map for output (print or export) rather than working only in the main map canvas.

trans-unit.source: The __Print Layout composer__:
trans-unit.target: The __Print Layout composer__:

trans-unit.source: Allows you to place map elements (map frames, legends, scale bars, titles, inset maps, annotations) in a fixed layout (page size, orientation, margins) suitable for export or printing.
trans-unit.target: Allows you to place map elements (map frames, legends, scale bars, titles, inset maps, annotations) in a fixed layout (page size, orientation, margins) suitable for export or printing.

trans-unit.source: The main QGIS map canvas is designed for interactive editing, exploration and dynamic symbology, not for designing a final composition with all the additional map elements and print specifications (paper size, resolution, export format).
trans-unit.target: The main QGIS map canvas is designed for interactive editing, exploration and dynamic symbology, not for designing a final composition with all the additional map elements and print specifications (paper size, resolution, export format).

trans-unit.source: In the print layout composer, you can precisely control placement, sizing, layering and placement of map elements.
trans-unit.target: In the print layout composer, you can precisely control placement, sizing, layering and placement of map elements.

trans-unit.source: It enables you to create a polished map product.
trans-unit.target: It enables you to create a polished map product.

trans-unit.source: __What are the key map elements (map components) you should include in a final layout (e.g. title, legend, north arrow, scale bar, inset, northing, attribution)?__
trans-unit.target: __What are the key map elements (map components) you should include in a final layout (e.g. title, legend, north arrow, scale bar, inset, northing, attribution)?__

trans-unit.source: :::{dropdown} Answer
trans-unit.target: :::{dropdown} Answer

trans-unit.source: A good map should offer additional information, that allows the contextualisation of the map and the displayed information as well as the attribution.
trans-unit.target: A good map should offer additional information, that allows the contextualisation of the map and the displayed information as well as the attribution.

trans-unit.source: The basic elements for a complete map layout should include:
trans-unit.target: The basic elements for a complete map layout should include:

trans-unit.source: __Title:__ A short, concise statement of what the map shows (including the place, the subject and the date if relevant).
trans-unit.target: __Title:__ A short, concise statement of what the map shows (including the place, the subject and the date if relevant).

trans-unit.source: __Legend:__ Explains the symbols, colours and layers used in the map so the reader can interpret the symbology in the map.
trans-unit.target: __Legend:__ Explains the symbols, colours and layers used in the map so the reader can interpret the symbology in the map.

trans-unit.source: __Scale:__ Indicates the spatial relationship between map units and real-world distances.
trans-unit.target: __Scale:__ Indicates the spatial relationship between map units and real-world distances.

trans-unit.source: __Orientation:__ Typically a north arrow.
trans-unit.target: __Orientation:__ Typically a north arrow.

trans-unit.source: __Source/Data attribution:__ A statement of the data sources, author, date of data.
trans-unit.target: __Source/Data attribution:__ A statement of the data sources, author, date of data.

trans-unit.source: Additional information to help contextualise the map (e.g., description, overview map, attribute table, graph,...) :::
trans-unit.target: Additional information to help contextualise the map (e.g., description, overview map, attribute table, graph,...) :::

trans-unit.source: Further Resources
trans-unit.target: Further Resources

trans-unit.source: [Data Visualization Accessibility Checklist](https://learn-sims.org/style-guidance/data-visualization-accessibility-checklist/)
trans-unit.target: [Data Visualization Accessibility Checklist](https://learn-sims.org/style-guidance/data-visualization-accessibility-checklist/)
